function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?

loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < sample' be skipped entirely?

dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 6:  <statement> int *restrict a1, *restrict a2, *restrict a3; </statement> is reached, the statement line 8:  <statement> a2 = (int*) malloc(size); </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 11:  <statement> a1[i] = a2[i] = 1; </statement> dominate line 21:  <statement> free(a1); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable a1 at line 7: <statement>   a1 = (int*) malloc(size); </statement> depend on the value of variable size at line 5: <statement>   size_t size = sample * sizeof(int); </statement>?
Does variable a3 at line 9: <statement>   a3 = (int*) malloc(size); </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 18: <statement>   for (int i = 0; i < sample; ++i) { </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'argc' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'sample' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “int *restrict a1, *restrict a2, *restrict a3;”?
